verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce ego suscitabo super Babylonem et super habitatores ejus,  qui cor suum levaverunt contra me,  quasi ventum pestilentem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנְנִי֙ מֵעִ֣יר עַל־בָּבֶ֔ל וְאֶל־יֹשְׁבֵ֖י לֵ֣ב קָמָ֑י ר֖וּחַ מַשְׁחִֽית׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord; Behold, I will raise up against Babylon, and against them that dwell in the midst of them that rise up against me, a destroying wind;

verses.latin_vulgate: et mittam in Babylonem ventilatores,  et ventilabunt eam et demolientur terram ejus,  quoniam venerunt super eam undique in die afflictionis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשִׁלַּחְתִּ֨י לְבָבֶ֤ל ׀ זָרִים֙ וְזֵר֔וּהָ וִיבֹקְק֖וּ אֶת־אַרְצָ֑הּ כִּי־הָי֥וּ עָלֶ֛יהָ מִסָּבִ֖יב בְּי֥וֹם רָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And will send unto Babylon fanners, that shall fan her, and shall empty her land: for in the day of trouble they shall be against her round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non tendat qui tendit arcum suum,  et non ascendat loricatus :  nolite parcere juvenibus ejus ;  interficite omnem militiam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶֽל־יִדְרֹ֤ךְ (ידרך) הַדֹּרֵךְ֙ קַשְׁתּ֔וֹ וְאֶֽל־יִתְעַ֖ל בְּסִרְיֹנ֑וֹ וְאַֽל־תַּחְמְלוּ֙ אֶל־בַּ֣חֻרֶ֔יהָ הַחֲרִ֖ימוּ כׇּל־צְבָאָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Against him that bendeth let the archer bend his bow, and against him that lifteth himself up in his brigandine: and spare ye not her young men; destroy ye utterly all her host.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cadent interfecti in terra Chaldæorum,  et vulnerati in regionibus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָפְל֥וּ חֲלָלִ֖ים בְּאֶ֣רֶץ כַּשְׂדִּ֑ים וּמְדֻקָּרִ֖ים בְּחוּצוֹתֶֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: Thus the slain shall fall in the land of the Chaldeans, and they that are thrust through in her streets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam non fuit viduatus Israël et Juda  a Deo suo, Domino exercituum,  terra autem eorum repleta est delicto  a Sancto Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֠י לֹא־אַלְמָ֨ן יִשְׂרָאֵ֤ל וִֽיהוּדָה֙ מֵאֱלֹהָ֔יו מֵיְהֹוָ֖ה צְבָא֑וֹת כִּ֤י אַרְצָם֙ מָלְאָ֣ה אָשָׁ֔ם מִקְּד֖וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: For Israel hath not been forsaken, nor Judah of his God, of the Lord of hosts; though their land was filled with sin against the Holy One of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugite de medio Babylonis,  et salvet unusquisque animam suam :  nolite tacere super iniquitatem ejus,  quoniam tempus ultionis est a Domino :  vicissitudinem ipse retribuet ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֻ֣סוּ ׀ מִתּ֣וֹךְ בָּבֶ֗ל וּמַלְּטוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ נַפְשׁ֔וֹ אַל־תִּדַּ֖מּוּ בַּעֲוֺנָ֑הּ כִּי֩ עֵ֨ת נְקָמָ֥ה הִיא֙ לַיהֹוָ֔ה גְּמ֕וּל ה֥וּא מְשַׁלֵּ֖ם לָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Flee out of the midst of Babylon, and deliver every man his soul: be not cut off in her iniquity; for this is the time of the Lord’s vengeance; he will render unto her a recompence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Calix aureus Babylon in manu Domini,  inebrians omnem terram :  de vino ejus biberunt gentes,  et ideo commotæ sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כּוֹס־זָהָ֤ב בָּבֶל֙ בְּיַד־יְהֹוָ֔ה מְשַׁכֶּ֖רֶת כׇּל־הָאָ֑רֶץ מִיֵּינָהּ֙ שָׁת֣וּ גוֹיִ֔ם עַל־כֵּ֖ן יִתְהֹלְל֥וּ גוֹיִֽם׃
verses.king_james: Babylon hath been a golden cup in the Lord’s hand, that made all the earth drunken: the nations have drunken of her wine; therefore the nations are mad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Subito cecidit Babylon, et contrita est.  Ululate super eam :  tollite resinam ad dolorem ejus,  si forte sanetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פִּתְאֹ֛ם נָפְלָ֥ה בָבֶ֖ל וַתִּשָּׁבֵ֑ר הֵילִ֣ילוּ עָלֶ֗יהָ קְח֤וּ צֳרִי֙ לְמַכְאוֹבָ֔הּ אוּלַ֖י תֵּרָפֵֽא׃
verses.king_james: Babylon is suddenly fallen and destroyed: howl for her; take balm for her pain, if so be she may be healed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Curavimus Babylonem, et non est sanata :  derelinquamus eam, et eamus unusquisque in terram suam :  quoniam pervenit usque ad cælos judicium ejus,  et elevatum est usque ad nubes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רִפִּ֤אנוּ אֶת־בָּבֶל֙ וְלֹ֣א נִרְפָּ֔תָה עִזְב֕וּהָ וְנֵלֵ֖ךְ אִ֣ישׁ לְאַרְצ֑וֹ כִּֽי־נָגַ֤ע אֶל־הַשָּׁמַ֙יִם֙ מִשְׁפָּטָ֔הּ וְנִשָּׂ֖א עַד־שְׁחָקִֽים׃
verses.king_james: We would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed: forsake her, and let us go every one into his own country: for her judgment reacheth unto heaven, and is lifted up even to the skies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Protulit Dominus justitias nostras :  venite, et narremus in Sion opus Domini Dei nostri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוֹצִ֥יא יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶת־צִדְקֹתֵ֑ינוּ בֹּ֚אוּ וּנְסַפְּרָ֣ה בְצִיּ֔וֹן אֶֽת־מַעֲשֵׂ֖ה יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The Lord hath brought forth our righteousness: come, and let us declare in Zion the work of the Lord our God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Acuite sagittas, implete pharetras :  suscitavit Dominus spiritum regum Medorum :  et contra Babylonem mens ejus est ut perdat eam,  quoniam ultio Domini est,  ultio templi sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָבֵ֣רוּ הַחִצִּים֮ מִלְא֣וּ הַשְּׁלָטִים֒ הֵעִ֣יר יְהֹוָ֗ה אֶת־ר֙וּחַ֙ מַלְכֵ֣י מָדַ֔י כִּֽי־עַל־בָּבֶ֥ל מְזִמָּת֖וֹ לְהַשְׁחִיתָ֑הּ כִּֽי־נִקְמַ֤ת יְהֹוָה֙ הִ֔יא נִקְמַ֖ת הֵיכָלֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Make bright the arrows; gather the shields: the Lord hath raised up the spirit of the kings of the Medes: for his device is against Babylon, to destroy it; because it is the vengeance of the Lord, the vengeance of his temple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super muros Babylonis levate signum,  augete custodiam, levate custodes,  præparate insidias :  quia cogitavit Dominus,  et fecit quæcumque locutus est contra habitatores Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶל־חוֹמֹ֨ת בָּבֶ֜ל שְׂאוּ־נֵ֗ס הַחֲזִ֙יקוּ֙ הַמִּשְׁמָ֔ר הָקִ֙ימוּ֙ שֹֽׁמְרִ֔ים הָכִ֖ינוּ הָאֹֽרְבִ֑ים כִּ֚י גַּם־זָמַ֣ם יְהֹוָ֔ה גַּם־עָשָׂ֕ה אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּ֖ר אֶל־יֹשְׁבֵ֥י בָבֶֽל׃
verses.king_james: Set up the standard upon the walls of Babylon, make the watch strong, set up the watchmen, prepare the ambushes: for the Lord hath both devised and done that which he spake against the inhabitants of Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ habitas super aquas multas,  locuples in thesauris :  venit finis tuus, pedalis præcisionis tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (שכנתי) [שֹׁכַנְתְּ֙] עַל־מַ֣יִם רַבִּ֔ים רַבַּ֖ת אוֹצָרֹ֑ת בָּ֥א קִצֵּ֖ךְ אַמַּ֥ת בִּצְעֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: O thou that dwellest upon many waters, abundant in treasures, thine end is come, and the measure of thy covetousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Juravit Dominus exercituum per animam suam :  Quoniam replebo te hominibus quasi brucho,  et super te celeuma cantabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִשְׁבַּ֛ע יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת בְּנַפְשׁ֑וֹ כִּ֣י אִם־מִלֵּאתִ֤יךְ אָדָם֙ כַּיֶּ֔לֶק וְעָנ֥וּ עָלַ֖יִךְ הֵידָֽד׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The Lord of hosts hath sworn by himself, saying, Surely I will fill thee with men, as with caterpillers; and they shall lift up a shout against thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui fecit terram in fortitudine sua,  præparavit orbem in sapientia sua,  et prudentia sua extendit cælos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹשֵׂ֥ה אֶ֙רֶץ֙ בְּכֹח֔וֹ מֵכִ֥ין תֵּבֵ֖ל בְּחׇכְמָת֑וֹ וּבִתְבוּנָת֖וֹ נָטָ֥ה שָׁמָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heaven by his understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dante eo vocem, multiplicantur aquæ in cælo :  qui levat nubes ab extremo terræ,  fulgura in pluviam fecit,  et produxit ventum de thesauris suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְק֨וֹל תִּתּ֜וֹ הֲמ֥וֹן מַ֙יִם֙ בַּשָּׁמַ֔יִם וַיַּ֥עַל נְשִׂאִ֖ים מִקְצֵה־אָ֑רֶץ בְּרָקִ֤ים לַמָּטָר֙ עָשָׂ֔ה וַיּ֥וֹצֵא ר֖וּחַ מֵאֹצְרֹתָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens; and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth: he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus factus est omnis homo a scientia ;  confusus est omnis conflator in sculptili :  quia mendax est conflatio eorum,  nec est spiritus in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִבְעַ֤ר כׇּל־אָדָם֙ מִדַּ֔עַת הֹבִ֥ישׁ כׇּל־צֹרֵ֖ף מִפָּ֑סֶל כִּ֛י שֶׁ֥קֶר נִסְכּ֖וֹ וְלֹא־ר֥וּחַ בָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Every man is brutish by his knowledge; every founder is confounded by the graven image: for his molten image is falsehood, and there is no breath in them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vana sunt opera, et risu digna :  in tempore visitationis suæ peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶ֣בֶל הֵ֔מָּה מַעֲשֵׂ֖ה תַּעְתֻּעִ֑ים בְּעֵ֥ת פְּקֻדָּתָ֖ם יֹאבֵֽדוּ׃
verses.king_james: They are vanity, the work of errors: in the time of their visitation they shall perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non sicut hæc, pars Jacob,  quia qui fecit omnia ipse est :  et Israël sceptrum hæreditatis ejus :  Dominus exercituum nomen ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־כְאֵ֜לֶּה חֵ֣לֶק יַעֲק֗וֹב כִּֽי־יוֹצֵ֤ר הַכֹּל֙ ה֔וּא וְשֵׁ֖בֶט נַחֲלָת֑וֹ יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: The portion of Jacob is not like them; for he is the former of all things: and Israel is the rod of his inheritance: the Lord of hosts is his name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Collidis tu mihi vasa belli :  et ego collidam in te gentes,  et disperdam in te regna :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַפֵּץ־אַתָּ֣ה לִ֔י כְּלֵ֖י מִלְחָמָ֑ה וְנִפַּצְתִּ֤י בְךָ֙ גּוֹיִ֔ם וְהִשְׁחַתִּ֥י בְךָ֖ מַמְלָכֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Thou art my battle axe and weapons of war: for with thee will I break in pieces the nations, and with thee will I destroy kingdoms;

verses.latin_vulgate: et collidam in te equum et equitem ejus :  et collidam in te currum et ascensorem ejus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִפַּצְתִּ֣י בְךָ֔ ס֖וּס וְרֹכְב֑וֹ וְנִפַּצְתִּ֣י בְךָ֔ רֶ֖כֶב וְרֹכְבֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And with thee will I break in pieces the horse and his rider; and with thee will I break in pieces the chariot and his rider;

verses.latin_vulgate: et collidam in te virum et mulierem :  et collidam in te senem et puerum :  et collidam in te juvenem et virginem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִפַּצְתִּ֤י בְךָ֙ אִ֣ישׁ וְאִשָּׁ֔ה וְנִפַּצְתִּ֥י בְךָ֖ זָקֵ֣ן וָנָ֑עַר וְנִפַּצְתִּ֣י בְךָ֔ בָּח֖וּר וּבְתוּלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: With thee also will I break in pieces man and woman; and with thee will I break in pieces old and young; and with thee will I break in pieces the young man and the maid;

verses.latin_vulgate: et collidam in te pastorem et gregem ejus :  et collidam in te agricolam et jugales ejus :  et collidam in te duces et magistratus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִפַּצְתִּ֤י בְךָ֙ רֹעֶ֣ה וְעֶדְר֔וֹ וְנִפַּצְתִּ֥י בְךָ֖ אִכָּ֣ר וְצִמְדּ֑וֹ וְנִפַּצְתִּ֣י בְךָ֔ פַּח֖וֹת וּסְגָנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: I will also break in pieces with thee the shepherd and his flock; and with thee will I break in pieces the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and with thee will I break in pieces captains and rulers.

verses.latin_vulgate: et reddam Babyloni, et cunctis habitatoribus Chaldææ,  omne malum suum quod fecerunt in Sion,  in oculis vestris, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשִׁלַּמְתִּ֨י לְבָבֶ֜ל וּלְכֹ֣ל ׀ יוֹשְׁבֵ֣י כַשְׂדִּ֗ים אֵ֧ת כׇּל־רָעָתָ֛ם אֲשֶׁר־עָשׂ֥וּ בְצִיּ֖וֹן לְעֵינֵיכֶ֑ם נְאֻ֖ם יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And I will render unto Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea all their evil that they have done in Zion in your sight, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego ad te, mons pestifer, ait Dominus,  qui corrumpis universam terram :  et extendam manum meam super te,  et evolvam te de petris,  et dabo te in montem combustionis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְנִ֨י אֵלֶ֜יךָ הַ֤ר הַמַּשְׁחִית֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה הַמַּשְׁחִ֖ית אֶת־כׇּל־הָאָ֑רֶץ וְנָטִ֨יתִי אֶת־יָדִ֜י עָלֶ֗יךָ וְגִלְגַּלְתִּ֙יךָ֙ מִן־הַסְּלָעִ֔ים וּנְתַתִּ֖יךָ לְהַ֥ר שְׂרֵפָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I am against thee, O destroying mountain, saith the Lord, which destroyest all the earth: and I will stretch out mine hand upon thee, and roll thee down from the rocks, and will make thee a burnt mountain.

verses.latin_vulgate: et non tollent de te lapidem in angulum,  et lapidem in fundamenta :  sed perditus in æternum eris, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹֽא־יִקְח֤וּ מִמְּךָ֙ אֶ֣בֶן לְפִנָּ֔ה וְאֶ֖בֶן לְמוֹסָד֑וֹת כִּֽי־שִׁמְמ֥וֹת עוֹלָ֛ם תִּהְיֶ֖ה נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And they shall not take of thee a stone for a corner, nor a stone for foundations; but thou shalt be desolate for ever, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levate signum in terra,  clangite buccina in gentibus,  sanctificate super eam gentes,  annuntiate contra illam regibus Ararat, Menni, et Ascenez :  numerate contra eam Taphsar,  adducite equum quasi bruchum aculeatum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂאוּ־נֵ֣ס בָּאָ֗רֶץ תִּקְע֨וּ שׁוֹפָ֤ר בַּגּוֹיִם֙ קַדְּשׁ֤וּ עָלֶ֙יהָ֙ גּוֹיִ֔ם הַשְׁמִ֧יעוּ עָלֶ֛יהָ מַמְלְכ֥וֹת אֲרָרַ֖ט מִנִּ֣י וְאַשְׁכְּנָ֑ז פִּקְד֤וּ עָלֶ֙יהָ֙ טִפְסָ֔ר הַעֲלוּ־ס֖וּס כְּיֶ֥לֶק סָמָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Set ye up a standard in the land, blow the trumpet among the nations, prepare the nations against her, call together against her the kingdoms of Ararat, Minni, and Ashchenaz; appoint a captain against her; cause the horses to come up as the rough caterpillers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sanctificate contra eam gentes,  reges Mediæ, duces ejus, et universos magistratus ejus,  cunctamque terram potestatis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קַדְּשׁ֨וּ עָלֶ֤יהָ גוֹיִם֙ אֶת־מַלְכֵ֣י מָדַ֔י אֶת־פַּחוֹתֶ֖יהָ וְאֶת־כׇּל־סְגָנֶ֑יהָ וְאֵ֖ת כׇּל־אֶ֥רֶץ מֶמְשַׁלְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Prepare against her the nations with the kings of the Medes, the captains thereof, and all the rulers thereof, and all the land of his dominion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et commovebitur terra et conturbabitur,  quia evigilabit contra Babylonem cogitatio Domini,  ut ponat terram Babylonis desertam et inhabitabilem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּרְעַ֥שׁ הָאָ֖רֶץ וַתָּחֹ֑ל כִּ֣י קָ֤מָה עַל־בָּבֶל֙ מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת יְהֹוָ֔ה לָשׂ֞וּם אֶת־אֶ֧רֶץ בָּבֶ֛ל לְשַׁמָּ֖ה מֵאֵ֥ין יוֹשֵֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: And the land shall tremble and sorrow: for every purpose of the Lord shall be performed against Babylon, to make the land of Babylon a desolation without an inhabitant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cessaverunt fortes Babylonis a prælio ;  habitaverunt in præsidiis :  devoratum est robur eorum,  et facti sunt quasi mulieres :  incensa sunt tabernacula ejus,  contriti sunt vectes ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָדְלוּ֩ גִבּוֹרֵ֨י בָבֶ֜ל לְהִלָּחֵ֗ם יָֽשְׁבוּ֙ בַּמְּצָד֔וֹת נָשְׁתָ֥ה גְבוּרָתָ֖ם הָי֣וּ לְנָשִׁ֑ים הִצִּ֥יתוּ מִשְׁכְּנֹתֶ֖יהָ נִשְׁבְּר֥וּ בְרִיחֶֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: The mighty men of Babylon have forborn to fight, they have remained in their holds: their might hath failed; they became as women: they have burned her dwellingplaces; her bars are broken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Currens obviam currenti veniet,  et nuntius obvius nuntianti,  ut annuntiet regi Babylonis  quia capta est civitas ejus a summo usque ad summum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָ֤ץ לִקְרַאת־רָץ֙ יָר֔וּץ וּמַגִּ֖יד לִקְרַ֣את מַגִּ֑יד לְהַגִּיד֙ לְמֶ֣לֶךְ בָּבֶ֔ל כִּֽי־נִלְכְּדָ֥ה עִיר֖וֹ מִקָּצֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: One post shall run to meet another, and one messenger to meet another, to shew the king of Babylon that his city is taken at one end,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vada præoccupata sunt,  et paludes incensæ sunt igni,  et viri bellatores conturbati sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמַּעְבָּר֣וֹת נִתְפָּ֔שׂוּ וְאֶת־הָאֲגַמִּ֖ים שָׂרְפ֣וּ בָאֵ֑שׁ וְאַנְשֵׁ֥י הַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה נִבְהָֽלוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And that the passages are stopped, and the reeds they have burned with fire, and the men of war are affrighted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël :  Filia Babylonis quasi area,  tempus trituræ ejus :  adhuc modicum, et veniet tempus messionis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי֩ כֹ֨ה אָמַ֜ר יְהֹוָ֤ה צְבָאוֹת֙ אֱלֹהֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל בַּת־בָּבֶ֕ל כְּגֹ֖רֶן עֵ֣ת הִדְרִיכָ֑הּ ע֣וֹד מְעַ֔ט וּבָ֥אָה עֵֽת־הַקָּצִ֖יר לָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: For thus saith the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel; The daughter of Babylon is like a threshingfloor, it is time to thresh her: yet a little while, and the time of her harvest shall come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Comedit me, devoravit me Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis :  reddidit me quasi vas inane,  absorbuit me quasi draco,  replevit ventrem suum teneritudine mea, et ejecit me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (אכלנו) [אֲכָלַ֣נִי] (הממנו) [הֲמָמַ֗נִי] נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּר֮ מֶ֣לֶךְ בָּבֶל֒ (הציגנו) [הִצִּיגַ֙נִי֙] כְּלִ֣י רִ֔יק (בלענו) [בְּלָעַ֙נִי֙] כַּתַּנִּ֔ין מִלָּ֥א כְרֵשׂ֖וֹ מֵעֲדָנָ֑י (הדיחנו) [הֱדִיחָֽנִי]׃
verses.king_james: Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon hath devoured me, he hath crushed me, he hath made me an empty vessel, he hath swallowed me up like a dragon, he hath filled his belly with my delicates, he hath cast me out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iniquitas adversum me  et caro mea super Babylonem,  dicit habitatio Sion :  et sanguis meus super habitatores Chaldææ, dicit Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲמָסִ֤י וּשְׁאֵרִי֙ עַל־בָּבֶ֔ל תֹּאמַ֖ר יֹשֶׁ֣בֶת צִיּ֑וֹן וְדָמִי֙ אֶל־יֹשְׁבֵ֣י כַשְׂדִּ֔ים תֹּאמַ֖ר יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon, shall the inhabitant of Zion say; and my blood upon the inhabitants of Chaldea, shall Jerusalem say.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce ego judicabo causam tuam,  et ulciscar ultionem tuam :  et desertum faciam mare ejus,  et siccabo venam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנְנִי־רָב֙ אֶת־רִיבֵ֔ךְ וְנִקַּמְתִּ֖י אֶת־נִקְמָתֵ֑ךְ וְהַֽחֲרַבְתִּי֙ אֶת־יַמָּ֔הּ וְהֹֽבַשְׁתִּ֖י אֶת־מְקוֹרָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore thus saith the Lord; Behold, I will plead thy cause, and take vengeance for thee; and I will dry up her sea, and make her springs dry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit Babylon in tumulos,  habitatio draconum, stupor et sibilus,  eo quod non sit habitator.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיְתָה֩ בָבֶ֨ל ׀ לְגַלִּ֧ים ׀ מְעוֹן־תַּנִּ֛ים שַׁמָּ֥ה וּשְׁרֵקָ֖ה מֵאֵ֥ין יוֹשֵֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: And Babylon shall become heaps, a dwellingplace for dragons, an astonishment, and an hissing, without an inhabitant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simul ut leones rugient ;  excutient comas veluti catuli leonum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַחְדָּ֖ו כַּכְּפִרִ֣ים יִשְׁאָ֑גוּ נָעֲר֖וּ כְּגוֹרֵ֥י אֲרָיֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: They shall roar together like lions: they shall yell as lions’ whelps.

verses.latin_vulgate: In calore eorum ponam potus eorum,  et inebriabo eos ut sopiantur,  et dormiant somnum sempiternum,  et non consurgant, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּחֻמָּ֞ם אָשִׁ֣ית אֶת־מִשְׁתֵּיהֶ֗ם וְהִשְׁכַּרְתִּים֙ לְמַ֣עַן יַעֲלֹ֔זוּ וְיָשְׁנ֥וּ שְׁנַת־עוֹלָ֖ם וְלֹ֣א יָקִ֑יצוּ נְאֻ֖ם יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: In their heat I will make their feasts, and I will make them drunken, that they may rejoice, and sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deducam eos quasi agnos ad victimam,  et quasi arietes cum hædis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אוֹרִידֵ֖ם כְּכָרִ֣ים לִטְב֑וֹחַ כְּאֵילִ֖ים עִם־עַתּוּדִֽים׃
verses.king_james: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with he goats.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo capta est Sesach,  et comprehensa est inclyta universæ terræ !  quomodo facta est in stuporem Babylon inter gentes !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֚יךְ נִלְכְּדָ֣ה שֵׁשַׁ֔ךְ וַתִּתָּפֵ֖שׂ תְּהִלַּ֣ת כׇּל־הָאָ֑רֶץ אֵ֣יךְ הָיְתָ֧ה לְשַׁמָּ֛ה בָּבֶ֖ל בַּגּוֹיִֽם׃
verses.king_james: How is Sheshach taken! and how is the praise of the whole earth surprised! how is Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit super Babylonem mare :  multitudine fluctuum ejus operta est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָלָ֥ה עַל־בָּבֶ֖ל הַיָּ֑ם בַּהֲמ֥וֹן גַּלָּ֖יו נִכְסָֽתָה׃
verses.king_james: The sea is come up upon Babylon: she is covered with the multitude of the waves thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factæ sunt civitates ejus in stuporem,  terra inhabitabilis et deserta,  terra in qua nullus habitet,  nec transeat per eam filius hominis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָי֤וּ עָרֶ֙יהָ֙ לְשַׁמָּ֔ה אֶ֖רֶץ צִיָּ֣ה וַעֲרָבָ֑ה אֶ֗רֶץ לֹֽא־יֵשֵׁ֤ב בָּהֵן֙ כׇּל־אִ֔ישׁ וְלֹא־יַעֲבֹ֥ר בָּהֵ֖ן בֶּן־אָדָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Her cities are a desolation, a dry land, and a wilderness, a land wherein no man dwelleth, neither doth any son of man pass thereby.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et visitabo super Bel in Babylone,  et ejiciam quod absorbuerat de ore ejus :  et non confluent ad eum ultra gentes,  siquidem et murus Babylonis corruet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּפָקַדְתִּ֨י עַל־בֵּ֜ל בְּבָבֶ֗ל וְהֹצֵאתִ֤י אֶת־בִּלְעוֹ֙ מִפִּ֔יו וְלֹא־יִנְהֲר֥וּ אֵלָ֛יו ע֖וֹד גּוֹיִ֑ם גַּם־חוֹמַ֥ת בָּבֶ֖ל נָפָֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: And I will punish Bel in Babylon, and I will bring forth out of his mouth that which he hath swallowed up: and the nations shall not flow together any more unto him: yea, the wall of Babylon shall fall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egredimini de medio ejus, populus meus,  ut salvet unusquisque animam suam ab ira furoris Domini,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צְא֤וּ מִתּוֹכָהּ֙ עַמִּ֔י וּמַלְּט֖וּ אִ֣ישׁ אֶת־נַפְשׁ֑וֹ מֵחֲר֖וֹן אַף־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: My people, go ye out of the midst of her, and deliver ye every man his soul from the fierce anger of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ne forte mollescat cor vestrum,  et timeatis auditum qui audietur in terra :  et veniet in anno auditio,  et post hunc annum auditio,  et iniquitas in terra,  et dominator super dominatorem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּפֶן־יֵרַ֤ךְ לְבַבְכֶם֙ וְתִֽירְא֔וּ בַּשְּׁמוּעָ֖ה הַנִּשְׁמַ֣עַת בָּאָ֑רֶץ וּבָ֧א בַשָּׁנָ֣ה הַשְּׁמוּעָ֗ה וְאַחֲרָ֤יו בַּשָּׁנָה֙ הַשְּׁמוּעָ֔ה וְחָמָ֣ס בָּאָ֔רֶץ וּמֹשֵׁ֖ל עַל־מֹשֵֽׁל׃
verses.king_james: And lest your heart faint, and ye fear for the rumour that shall be heard in the land; a rumour shall both come one year, and after that in another year shall come a rumour, and violence in the land, ruler against ruler.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea ecce dies veniunt,  et visitabo super sculptilia Babylonis,  et omnis terra ejus confundetur,  et universi interfecti ejus cadent in medio ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵן֙ הִנֵּ֣ה יָמִ֣ים בָּאִ֔ים וּפָֽקַדְתִּי֙ עַל־פְּסִילֵ֣י בָבֶ֔ל וְכׇל־אַרְצָ֖הּ תֵּב֑וֹשׁ וְכׇל־חֲלָלֶ֖יהָ יִפְּל֥וּ בְתוֹכָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore, behold, the days come, that I will do judgment upon the graven images of Babylon: and her whole land shall be confounded, and all her slain shall fall in the midst of her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et laudabunt super Babylonem cæli et terra,  et omnia quæ in eis sunt :  quia ab aquilone venient ei prædones, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרִנְּנ֤וּ עַל־בָּבֶל֙ שָׁמַ֣יִם וָאָ֔רֶץ וְכֹ֖ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר בָּהֶ֑ם כִּ֧י מִצָּפ֛וֹן יָבוֹא־לָ֥הּ הַשּׁוֹדְדִ֖ים נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Then the heaven and the earth, and all that is therein, shall sing for Babylon: for the spoilers shall come unto her from the north, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quomodo fecit Babylon,  ut caderent occisi in Israël,  sic de Babylone  cadent occisi in universa terra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־בָּבֶ֕ל לִנְפֹּ֖ל חַֽלְלֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל גַּם־לְבָבֶ֥ל נָפְל֖וּ חַֽלְלֵ֥י כׇל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: As Babylon hath caused the slain of Israel to fall, so at Babylon shall fall the slain of all the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui fugistis gladium, venite,  nolite stare :  recordamini procul Domini,  et Jerusalem ascendat super cor vestrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּלֵטִ֣ים מֵחֶ֔רֶב הִלְכ֖וּ אַֽל־תַּעֲמֹ֑דוּ זִכְר֤וּ מֵרָחוֹק֙ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֔ה וִירוּשָׁלַ֖͏ִם תַּעֲלֶ֥ה עַל־לְבַבְכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Ye that have escaped the sword, go away, stand not still: remember the Lord afar off, and let Jerusalem come into your mind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confusi sumus, quoniam audivimus opprobrium :  operuit ignominia facies nostras,  quia venerunt alieni super sanctificationem domus Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֹּ֚שְׁנוּ כִּֽי־שָׁמַ֣עְנוּ חֶרְפָּ֔ה כִּסְּתָ֥ה כְלִמָּ֖ה פָּנֵ֑ינוּ כִּ֚י בָּ֣אוּ זָרִ֔ים עַֽל־מִקְדְּשֵׁ֖י בֵּ֥ית יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: We are confounded, because we have heard reproach: shame hath covered our faces: for strangers are come into the sanctuaries of the Lord’s house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea ecce dies veniunt, ait Dominus,  et visitabo super sculptilia ejus,  et in omni terra ejus mugiet vulneratus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֞ן הִנֵּֽה־יָמִ֤ים בָּאִים֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה וּפָקַדְתִּ֖י עַל־פְּסִילֶ֑יהָ וּבְכׇל־אַרְצָ֖הּ יֶאֱנֹ֥ק חָלָֽל׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore, behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will do judgment upon her graven images: and through all her land the wounded shall groan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ascenderit Babylon in cælum,  et firmaverit in excelso robur suum,  a me venient vastatores ejus, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־תַעֲלֶ֤ה בָבֶל֙ הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וְכִ֥י תְבַצֵּ֖ר מְר֣וֹם עֻזָּ֑הּ מֵאִתִּ֗י יָבֹ֧אוּ שֹׁדְדִ֛ים לָ֖הּ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Though Babylon should mount up to heaven, and though she should fortify the height of her strength, yet from me shall spoilers come unto her, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox clamoris de Babylone,  et contritio magna de terra Chaldæorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֥וֹל זְעָקָ֖ה מִבָּבֶ֑ל וְשֶׁ֥בֶר גָּד֖וֹל מֵאֶ֥רֶץ כַּשְׂדִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: A sound of a cry cometh from Babylon, and great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans:

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam vastavit Dominus Babylonem,  et perdidit ex ea vocem magnam :  et sonabunt fluctus eorum quasi aquæ multæ ;  dedit sonitum vox eorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־שֹׁדֵ֤ד יְהֹוָה֙ אֶת־בָּבֶ֔ל וְאִבַּ֥ד מִמֶּ֖נָּה ק֣וֹל גָּד֑וֹל וְהָמ֤וּ גַלֵּיהֶם֙ כְּמַ֣יִם רַבִּ֔ים נִתַּ֥ן שְׁא֖וֹן קוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Because the Lord hath spoiled Babylon, and destroyed out of her the great voice; when her waves do roar like great waters, a noise of their voice is uttered:

verses.latin_vulgate: quia venit super eam, id est super Babylonem, prædo,  et apprehensi sunt fortes ejus,  et emarcuit arcus eorum,  quia fortis ultor Dominus reddens retribuet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי֩ בָ֨א עָלֶ֤יהָ עַל־בָּבֶל֙ שׁוֹדֵ֔ד וְנִלְכְּדוּ֙ גִּבּוֹרֶ֔יהָ חִתְּתָ֖ה קַשְּׁתוֹתָ֑ם כִּ֣י אֵ֧ל גְּמֻל֛וֹת יְהֹוָ֖ה שַׁלֵּ֥ם יְשַׁלֵּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Because the spoiler is come upon her, even upon Babylon, and her mighty men are taken, every one of their bows is broken: for the Lord God of recompences shall surely requite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inebriabo principes ejus, et sapientes ejus,  et duces ejus, et magistratus ejus, et fortes ejus :  et dormient somnum sempiternum,  et non expergiscentur, ait Rex  (Dominus exercituum nomen ejus).
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֠הִשְׁכַּרְתִּ֠י שָׂרֶ֨יהָ וַחֲכָמֶ֜יהָ פַּחוֹתֶ֤יהָ וּסְגָנֶ֙יהָ֙ וְגִבּוֹרֶ֔יהָ וְיָשְׁנ֥וּ שְׁנַת־עוֹלָ֖ם וְלֹ֣א יָקִ֑יצוּ נְאֻ֨ם־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת שְׁמֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And I will make drunk her princes, and her wise men, her captains, and her rulers, and her mighty men: and they shall sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum :  Murus Babylonis ille latissimus suffossione suffodietur,  et portæ ejus excelsæ igni comburentur,  et labores populorum ad nihilum,  et gentium in ignem erunt, et disperibunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּֽה־אָמַ֞ר יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֗וֹת חֹ֠מ֠וֹת בָּבֶ֤ל הָרְחָבָה֙ עַרְעֵ֣ר תִּתְעַרְעָ֔ר וּשְׁעָרֶ֥יהָ הַגְּבֹהִ֖ים בָּאֵ֣שׁ יִצַּ֑תּוּ וְיִֽגְע֨וּ עַמִּ֧ים בְּדֵי־רִ֛יק וּלְאֻמִּ֥ים בְּדֵי־אֵ֖שׁ וְיָעֵֽפוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord of hosts; The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly broken, and her high gates shall be burned with fire; and the people shall labour in vain, and the folk in the fire, and they shall be weary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verbum quod præcepit Jeremias propheta Saraiæ filio Neriæ filii Maasiæ, cum pergeret cum Sedecia rege in Babylonem, in anno quarto regni ejus : Saraias autem erat princeps prophetiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַדָּבָ֞ר אֲשֶׁר־צִוָּ֣ה ׀ יִרְמְיָ֣הוּ הַנָּבִ֗יא אֶת־שְׂרָיָ֣ה בֶן־נֵרִיָּה֮ בֶּן־מַחְסֵיָה֒ בְּלֶכְתּ֞וֹ אֶת־צִדְקִיָּ֤הוּ מֶֽלֶךְ־יְהוּדָה֙ בָּבֶ֔ל בִּשְׁנַ֥ת הָרְבִעִ֖ית לְמׇלְכ֑וֹ וּשְׂרָיָ֖ה שַׂ֥ר מְנוּחָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah, the son of Maaseiah, when he went with Zedekiah the king of Judah into Babylon in the fourth year of his reign. And this Seraiah was a quiet prince.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et scripsit Jeremias omne malum quod venturum erat super Babylonem, in libro uno : omnia verba hæc quæ scripta sunt contra Babylonem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּכְתֹּ֣ב יִרְמְיָ֗הוּ אֵ֧ת כׇּל־הָרָעָ֛ה אֲשֶׁר־תָּב֥וֹא אֶל־בָּבֶ֖ל אֶל־סֵ֣פֶר אֶחָ֑ד אֵ֚ת כׇּל־הַדְּבָרִ֣ים הָאֵ֔לֶּה הַכְּתֻבִ֖ים אֶל־בָּבֶֽל׃
verses.king_james: So Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon, even all these words that are written against Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Jeremias ad Saraiam : Cum veneris in Babylonem, et videris, et legeris omnia verba hæc,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יִרְמְיָ֖הוּ אֶל־שְׂרָיָ֑ה כְּבֹאֲךָ֣ בָבֶ֔ל וְֽרָאִ֔יתָ וְֽקָרָ֔אתָ אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הַדְּבָרִ֖ים הָאֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.king_james: And Jeremiah said to Seraiah, When thou comest to Babylon, and shalt see, and shalt read all these words;

verses.latin_vulgate: dices : Domine, tu locutus es contra locum istum, ut disperderes eum, ne sit qui in eo habitet, ab homine usque ad pecus, et ut sit perpetua solitudo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֗ יְהֹוָה֙ אַתָּ֨ה דִבַּ֜רְתָּ אֶל־הַמָּק֤וֹם הַזֶּה֙ לְהַכְרִית֔וֹ לְבִלְתִּ֤י הֱיֽוֹת־בּוֹ֙ יוֹשֵׁ֔ב לְמֵאָדָ֖ם וְעַד־בְּהֵמָ֑ה כִּֽי־שִׁמְמ֥וֹת עוֹלָ֖ם תִּהְיֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Then shalt thou say, O Lord, thou hast spoken against this place, to cut it off, that none shall remain in it, neither man nor beast, but that it shall be desolate for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque compleveris legere librum istum, ligabis ad eum lapidem, et projicies illum in medium Euphraten,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֙ כְּכַלֹּ֣תְךָ֔ לִקְרֹ֖א אֶת־הַסֵּ֣פֶר הַזֶּ֑ה תִּקְשֹׁ֤ר עָלָיו֙ אֶ֔בֶן וְהִשְׁלַכְתּ֖וֹ אֶל־תּ֥וֹךְ פְּרָֽת׃
verses.king_james: And it shall be, when thou hast made an end of reading this book, that thou shalt bind a stone to it, and cast it into the midst of Euphrates:

verses.latin_vulgate: et dices : Sic submergetur Babylon, et non consurget a facie afflictionis quam ego adduco super eam, et dissolvetur. Hucusque verba Jeremiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֗ כָּ֠כָה תִּשְׁקַ֨ע בָּבֶ֤ל וְלֹֽא־תָקוּם֙ מִפְּנֵ֣י הָרָעָ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֧ר אָנֹכִ֛י מֵבִ֥יא עָלֶ֖יהָ וְיָעֵ֑פוּ עַד־הֵ֖נָּה דִּבְרֵ֥י יִרְמְיָֽהוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And thou shalt say, Thus shall Babylon sink, and shall not rise from the evil that I will bring upon her: and they shall be weary. Thus far are the words of Jeremiah.
